Directions

## Important message

You must ONLY edit the part of this page labelled 'By what date must they comply?'
You are directing the Home Office to supply their documents and evidence.

Upload any case documents and evidence supplied by the respondent.

#### Advice on uploads
- Files must be no more than 100MB in size.
- Upload a Word doc or PDF file
- Before uploading a file, give it a meaningful file name. For example, respondent-evidence.pdf.
Upload case documents

#### Files should be:
- a maximum of 100MB in size (larger files must be split)
- labelled clearly, e.g. HObundle.pdf
- in Word or PDF format - other file formats must be sent to both the appellant and Tribunal
Upload Home Office bundle
You must now build your case by uploading your appeal argument and evidence.
Request to review the Home Office response

You must provide all the information for the Home Office to conduct a thorough review of their decision at this stage.

## 1. Download the template below and use it to create your appeal skeleton argument.

Legal_Argument_Template_10_11_18.doc

Advice: Your appeal skeleton argument must explain why this appeal should be allowed. It must comply with the practice directions.

## 2. Upload your appeal skeleton argument below.

#### Advice on uploads
- Files must be no more than 100MB in size.
- Upload a Word doc or PDF file
- Before uploading a file, give it a meaningful file name. For example, JSmith-Skeleton.pdf.
### 3. Upload the evidence upon which you rely, including any witness statements.
Appeal skeleton argument
Description
Evidence
#### You must upload your appeal skeleton argument to submit your case
You are directing the respondent to review and respond to the appeal skeleton argument.

Anything you put here will be added to the appeal tab. The legal representative will be notified of the update.

## Upload the appeal response as a document

#### Advice on uploads
- Files must be no more than 100MB in size.
- Upload a Word doc or PDF file
- Before uploading a file, give it a meaningful file name. For example, appeal_response.pdf.
Response document
Description
Evidence

#### Files should be:
- a maximum of 100MB in size (larger files must be split)
- labelled clearly, e.g. HOappealresponse.pdf
- in Word or PDF format - other file formats must be sent to both the appellant and Tribunal
Upload the appeal response
Describe the document
HintText: For example, Home Office response to appeal

Label: Add any additional evidence here
[Upload additional evidence](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/uploadAdditionalEvidence)

Additional evidence is anything submitted after the appeal skeleton argument.

#### Advice on uploads
- Files must be no more than 100MB in size.
- Upload a Word doc or PDF file
- Before uploading a file, give it a meaningful file name. For example, health-report-JSmith.pdf.
Upload documents
Add the hearing details below.
Hearing centre
Length of hearing
Hearing date and time

Enter the agreed hearing requirements in the fields below.
Don’t enter the reason the appellant made the request. The respondent will be shown this information and they must not be told of the appellant’s personal circumstances.
Adjustments to accommodate vulnerabilities
HintText: If the appellant says they have any personal vulnerabilities, state what adjustments the Tribunal will offer.

Label: Multimedia equipment
HintText: If the appellant needs multimedia equipment, state what the Tribunal will provide.

Label: Single-sex court
HintText: If the appellant asked for a single-sex court, state which gender was requested and whether the Tribunal can accommodate this.

Label: In camera court
HintText: If the appellant asked for an in camera court, state whether the Tribunal can accommodate this.

Label: Other adjustments
HintText: If the appellant made any other requests, state what adjustments the Tribunal will offer.

Label:
## Create a case summary and upload it below

Use the case summary template provided to you.

You must use this template to summarise the most relevant information in the case. This will be used by all parties in the hearing. You do not need to complete all the fields in the template.

Give the file a meaningful name before you upload it. For example, PA-54321-2019-Smith-CaseSummary.PDF

#### Upload your case summary below
Case summary document
Case summary description

## Write a brief introduction to the case

This can cover:
- the appellant's country of origin
- their date of birth
- the decision they are appealing against
Introduction

## Add the appellant's case summary

Go to the documents tab and find the appeal skeleton argument for this case. This should contain the case summary written by the appellant's legal representative. You can copy and paste this case summary into the box below.
Appellant's case summary
Do both parties agree the immigration history?
Agreed immigration history
If the parties do not agree on the immigration history, you must give the respondent's accounts of the immigration history and the areas of disagreement.
Give the respondent's account of the immigration history
Give the areas of disagreement in relation to immigration history
Do both parties agree the schedule of issues?

Go to the documents tab and find the appeal skeleton argument for this case. This should contain the schedule of issues written by the appellant's legal representative. You can copy and paste the schedule of issues into the box below.
The appellant's schedule of issues

Any discussion of the schedule of issues is likely to be found in the document tab. You should check the appeal skeleton argument and the Home Office's response and include the relevant information in the boxes below.
The appellant's schedule of issues as stated in the appeal skeleton argument
Areas of disagreement between the parties concerning the appellant's schedule of issues
### Are you giving an anonymity direction?
Anonymity direction
### Give the names of the legal representatives in this case
Legal representative for the appellant
Legal representative for the respondent

## What is your decision?  

If the appeal is dismissed on all grounds, select 'Dismissed on all grounds'. If the appeal is allowed on any grounds, you must select 'Allowed'.
Decision

Decision and reasons

- You can only apply a fee award when you have allowed the appeal

# Overview

![Appeal submitted](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_appealSubmitted.png)

## Do this next
You have submitted your appeal. A case officer will now review your appeal. You don't need to do anything else right now.

# Overview

## Current progress of the case

![Awaiting respondent evidence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_awaitingRespondentEvidence.png)

## Do this next
You'll get an email when the respondent evidence is available in the documents tab. You can start gathering your evidence now and building your case.

[Build your case](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/buildCase)

# Overview

## Current progress of the case

![Case building](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_caseBuilding.png)

## Do this next
The respondent evidence is now available in the documents tab. You now need to build your case.

[Build your case](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/buildCase)

# Overview

## Current progress of the case

![Case building](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_caseBuilding.png)

## Do this next
If you're ready for your case to be reviewed, [submit your case](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/submitCase).

If you're not yet ready for your case to be reviewed, continue to [build your case](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/buildCase).

# Overview

## Current progress of the case

![Case under review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_caseUnderReview.png)

## Do this next
Your case is now under review. You'll get an email telling you what happens next.

# Overview

## Current progress of the case

![Listing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_listing.png)

## Do this next
The case officer is reviewing the hearing requirements. Once the requirements have been agreed with the Tribunal, the case will be listed and you will receive a notification with the hearing notice.

# Overview ## Current progress of the case ![Prepare for hearing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_listed.png) ## Do this next

The case has now been listed. Go to the documents tab to see the hearing notice, which includes the hearing details.

The case officer will prepare the hearing bundle. You'll receive a notification when it is available to view in the documents tab.

[Go to the documents tab](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/#documents)

# Overview

## Current progress of the case

![Respondent review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_respondentReview.png)

## Do this next
The case has now been sent to the respondent for review.

When they've reviewed the case, we'll add their response to the documents tab. You'll get an email letting you know when it's there.

If you want to reply to the response, you should contact the case officer within 5 days. If you don't respond within 5 days, the case will proceed to a hearing.

# Overview ## Current progress of the case ![Respondent review](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/legalRep_decided.png) ## Do this next The case has been decided. You can now view the decision and reasons in the documents tab.

Either party can appeal the decision by making an application to the Upper Tribunal. If you decide to appeal, you must do this within 14 days of the judge’s signature on the decision.

To submit an appeal, you will need to complete form IAFT-4: First-tier Tribunal Application for Permission to Appeal to Upper Tribunal.

## Current progress of the case

![Appeal submitted](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/caseOfficer_appealSubmitted.png)

[Request respondent evidence](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/requestRespondentEvidence)

# Overview ## Current progress of the case ![Listing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/caseOfficer_listing.png) ## Do this next The legal representative will send you the completed hearing requirements document by email.

Add any comments or edits, and then sign the document to confirm the agreed hearing requirements.

Email the completed hearing requirements document to IACReformlisting@justice.gov.uk so that the case can be listed.

You’ll be notified when the case is listed and be able to view the hearing notice in the documents tab.

# Overview ## Current progress of the case ![Prepare for hearing](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/hmcts/ia-appeal-frontend/master/app/assets/images/caseOfficer_decided.png) ## Do this next The case has been decided. ## Appellant's details
Internal flag managed by API to enable/disable uploadAddendumEvidenceAction (inline action)
Evidence supplied after the hearing bundle
[Add additional evidence as an addendum](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/uploadAddendumEvidence)
Add the evidence and the context. This evidence will not be included in the hearing bundle, it will be added as an addendum.

If you have evidence from both the appellant and the respondent, submit these seperately.

#### Advice on uploads
- Files must be no more than 100MB in size.
- You can upload jpg, png, svg, gif, doc and PDF files.
- Before uploading a file, give it a meaningful file name. For example, health-report-Jsmith.pdf.

#### Upload evidence
Supplied by
You’ll need to explain why this evidence is being submitted after the hearing bundle has been produced.
Evidence

Applications

The appellant or the respondent has made an application. Record the reasons and the outcome of the application. If the application has been granted, you may need to edit listing, edit direction due date or end the appeal.
Attach a copy of the application email and any other communication.

# Overview
## Do this next
The application decision has been recorded and is now available in the applications tab. You must now [end the appeal](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/endAppeal).
Internal flag managed by API to enable/disable application time extension

# Overview
## Do this next
The application decision has been recorded and is now available in the applications tab. You must change the direction due date to send an updated direction. You will need to find the direction in the list of directions and only edit the due date.
Internal flag managed by API to enable/disable application edit listing

# Overview

The application decision has been recorded and is now available in the applications tab. Contact the listing team to relist the case. Once the case has been relisted, a new hearing notice will be issued.

## Do this next
The judge is writing the decisions and reasons. You will be notified when it is available to view.
[Upload additional evidence](/case/IA/Asylum/${[CASE_REFERENCE]}/trigger/uploadAdditionalEvidenceHomeOffice)
Internal flag managed by API to enable/disable uploadAdditionalEvidenceAction (inline action)

#### Files should be:
- a maximum of 100MB in size (larger file must be split) 
- labelled clearly, e.g. MedicalRecord.pdf
- in Word or PDF format – other file formats must be sent to both the appelant and Tribunal

You’ll need to explain why this evidence is being submitted late.
Additional evidence
